sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presented with neck pain that started after a car accident approximately one week ago.
He describes the pain as a 7 out of 10, not constant, but worsening with neck movement.
He has tried ibuprofen, which provided some relief, but not significantly.
He reports pain in the back of his neck, with worsening pain on extension and lateral bending, but no pain in the upper back or shoulders.
He denies headaches, hearing problems, or visual disturbances.
He has a history of playing football, but no prior neck injuries.
He did not seek medical attention after the accident due to cost concerns.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, with worsening pain on flexion, extension, and lateral bending.
He had limited range of motion in the neck.
Test Results: The patient's x-ray results showed no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neck strain, likely due to whiplash from the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; suspected concussion; whiplash
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for inflammation, and Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to avoid straining his neck and to be conservative with his movements.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any nerve impingement or other complications.
I referred the patient to either physical rehabilitation or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment after the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after receiving the MRI results to discuss further treatment and management.
